requirement: bootstrap requirement covered by linter unit tests
WBXML version byte is 0x03 for WBXML 1.3
WBXML publicid is encoded as mb_u_int32 or as 0 followed by STR_T offset
WBXML charset is encoded as mb_u_int32 IANA MIBenum (106 = UTF-8)
WBXML string-table is mb_u_int32 length followed by raw bytes
mb_u_int32 is big-endian 7-bit groups with continuation bit 0x80
mb_u_int32 round-trip boundaries 0; 0x7F; 0x80; 0x3FFF; 0x4000; 0x1FFFFF; 0xFFFFFFFF
SWITCH_PAGE token = 0x00 followed by 1 byte page index
END token = 0x01 closes the most recent open element
STR_I token = 0x03 followed by NUL-terminated UTF-8 string
STR_T token = 0x83 followed by mb_u_int32 string-table offset
OPAQUE token = 0xC3 followed by mb_u_int32 length and that many raw bytes
tag byte bit7 = has content; bit6 = has attributes; bits0-5 = tag identity within the active code page
Code page 0 carries AirSync element tags including Sync
Code page 1 carries Contacts element tags including FirstName/LastName/Email1Address
Code page 2 carries Email element tags including Subject/From/To/DateReceived
Code page 3 (AirNotify) reserved for legacy compatibility
Code page 4 carries Calendar element tags including StartTime/EndTime/Subject
Code page 5 carries MoveItems element tags
Code page 6 carries GetItemEstimate element tags
Code page 7 carries FolderHierarchy element tags including FolderSync/FolderCreate
Code page 8 carries MeetingResponse element tags
Code page 9 carries Tasks element tags including Subject/StartDate/DueDate
Code page 10 carries ResolveRecipients element tags
Code page 11 carries ValidateCert element tags
Code page 12 carries Contacts2 element tags including IMAddress/CustomerId
Code page 13 carries Ping element tags including HeartbeatInterval/Folders
Code page 14 carries Provision element tags including PolicyKey/EASProvisionDoc
Code page 15 carries Search element tags
Code page 16 carries GAL element tags
Code page 17 carries AirSyncBase element tags including Body/BodyPreference/Attachments
Code page 18 carries Settings element tags
Code page 19 carries DocumentLibrary element tags
Code page 20 carries ItemOperations element tags
Code page 21 carries ComposeMail element tags including SendMail/SmartReply/SmartForward
Code page 22 carries Email2 element tags including UmCallerID/UmUserNotes
Code page 23 carries Notes element tags including Subject/MessageClass
Code page 24 carries RightsManagement element tags including RightsManagementSupport/RightsManagementLicense
every tag in every code page has a unique 6-bit token within that page and unique name; lookups round-trip both ways
Encoder writes a tag byte composed from current code page identity and content/attribute bits
Encoder emits SWITCH_PAGE 0x00 followed by 1 byte page id when the active code page changes between tags
Encoder emits inline strings as STR_I 0x03 followed by UTF-8 bytes terminated by NUL
Encoder emits opaque payloads as OPAQUE 0xC3 followed by mb_u_int32 length and raw bytes
Encoder emits END 0x01 to close the most recent open element
Decoder yields tag tokens already resolved to (code page id; identity; attribute bit; content bit)
Decoder transparently consumes SWITCH_PAGE bytes and reflects the new page on subsequent tag tokens
Decoder reads STR_I as UTF-8 NUL-terminated string and STR_T as offset into the document string-table
Decoder reads OPAQUE payloads as a length-prefixed byte slice
Marshal resolves struct tag wbxml=Page.Tag via the registered code page tables
Marshal honours the ;omitempty option and skips zero-valued fields
Marshal honours the ;opaque option and emits the field value as OPAQUE
Marshal emits one element per slice entry preserving order
Marshal followed by Unmarshal reproduces the input struct verbatim including nested page changes
Decoder must not panic on any byte input regardless of malformed structure
Request line uses POST /Microsoft-Server-ActiveSync
Base64 query bytes layout: ProtocolVersion (1B); CommandCode (1B); Locale little-endian (2B); DeviceID len-prefixed; PolicyKey len-prefixed; DeviceType len-prefixed; Params
golden round-trip of canonical example bytes 8C 00 09 04 0A v140Device 00 0A SmartPhone matches base64 jAAJBAp2MTQwRGV2aWNlAApTbWFydFBob25l
Command-specific parameters use 1B tag + 1B length + value
Plain query carries Cmd; User; DeviceId; DeviceType as URL key=value pairs
Command codes Sync=0; FolderSync=9; Ping=18; Provision=20
MS-ASProtocolVersion header is mandatory and equals the protocol version e.g. 14.1
Content-Type is application/vnd.ms-sync.wbxml for WBXML payloads
User-Agent header is mandatory and identifies the client
X-MS-PolicyKey header carries the active provisioning policy key (0 for initial Provision)
Accept-Language header is honoured when set by the caller
Authorization Basic header carries username:password base64-encoded
PolicyStore Get/Set persist the current policy key across calls
SyncStateStore Get/Set persist the current SyncKey per collection
